preamble: The shift from the voiced aspirated alveolar stop [dʱ] to the voiced alveolar fricative [z] represents assibilation and lenition. This process often proceeds through intermediate stages like [ð] before stabilizing as the sibilant [z], documented in Indo-Aryan language families.

examples.note: In Kashmiri and other Dardic languages, historical voiced aspirates underwent affrication and spirantization to fricatives.
